questions.question: Who is Griffin in the story?
questions.answer: Griffin is a scientist who discovers how to make himself invisible.

questions.question: What extraordinary discovery does Griffin make?
questions.answer: Griffin discovers the secret of invisibility.

questions.question: Why did Griffin set fire to his landlord’s house?
questions.answer: He set fire to the house to take revenge on his landlord.

questions.question: Why did Griffin have to remove his clothes?
questions.answer: He had to remove his clothes because they did not become invisible.

questions.question: Why were Griffin’s footprints visible?
questions.answer: His footprints were visible because his feet were muddy.

questions.question: Why was winter a problem for Griffin?
questions.answer: Winter was a problem because Griffin could not wear clothes to keep warm.

questions.question: Why did Griffin enter a large store in London?
questions.answer: He entered the store to get warmth, food, and clothes.

questions.question: Where did Griffin go after leaving London?
questions.answer: Griffin went to the village of Iping.

questions.question: Who is Mrs Hall?
questions.answer: Mrs Hall is the wife of the innkeeper at Iping.

questions.question: Why did Mrs Hall think Griffin was eccentric?
questions.answer: She thought he was eccentric because of his strange appearance and behaviour.

questions.question: What strange incident occurred in the clergyman’s house?
questions.answer: Money was stolen though the room appeared empty.

questions.question: What strange events happened in Mrs Hall’s inn?
questions.answer: Furniture moved on its own without any visible person.

questions.question: Why did villagers begin to suspect Griffin?
questions.answer: They suspected him because he suddenly had money.

questions.question: Who was Mr Jaffers?
questions.answer: Mr Jaffers was the village constable.

questions.question: What happened when Griffin removed his disguise?
questions.answer: People saw a man without a visible head.

questions.question: Why could Mr Jaffers not arrest Griffin?
questions.answer: Griffin became completely invisible and escaped.

questions.question: What kind of person was Griffin?
questions.answer: Griffin was a lawless and selfish person.

questions.question: What genre does the story belong to?
questions.answer: The story belongs to the science fiction genre.

questions.question: What fear did Griffin create among villagers?
questions.answer: He created fear and panic among the villagers.

questions.question: Why did Griffin want to live in solitude?
questions.answer: He wanted solitude to hide his invisibility.

questions.question: What is the central theme of the story?
questions.answer: The central theme is the misuse of science.

questions.question: What message does the story convey?
questions.answer: The story conveys that science must be used responsibly.

questions.question: What does invisibility symbolise in the story?
questions.answer: Invisibility symbolises power without responsibility.

questions.question: Why did Griffin fail despite his intelligence?
questions.answer: He failed because he lacked moral responsibility.

questions.question: What lesson does ‘Footprints Without Feet’ teach?
questions.answer: It teaches that knowledge without ethics leads to destruction.
